summary: Import-time machine-id-keyed AES payload executed via new Function()
details: On require('@gbrlxvii/ts-project-lint'), index.js loads lib/perf.js, which reads /etc/machine-id and uses its bytes as an AES-128-CBC key (with a hardcoded IV b1abd372db55d68bd59310e9aa8e1d47) to decrypt a multi-kilobyte hex-encoded ciphertext blob. The resulting plaintext is then executed via `new Function(r)()` at module load time. The file exports an empty object — it has no legitimate API surface and exists solely to decrypt-and-eval the embedded blob. The package self-advertises as a TypeScript naming-convention linter; machine-id-keyed payload decryption and eval is unrelated to that purpose and matches the canonical machine-locked obfuscated dropper shape. The machine-id keying ensures the payload is only executable on the targeted host(s), evading sandbox analysis and indicating deliberate operational targeting. Any consumer who installs and require()s this package executes attacker-controlled code on import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
